claim_text: Patient woke up with shortness of breath.
Patient still has shortness of breath after going home.
Patient has been experiencing shortness of breath for two weeks.
Patient has been coughing up blood for the past three weeks.
Patient has a history of diabetes.
Patient has a history of high blood pressure.
Patient has a history of asthma.
Patient coughs up blood 3-4 times a day.
Patient has leg pain in the knee.
Patient has shortness of breath.
Patient has no fever.
Patient's blood sugar has been running high.
Patient's blood pressure has been running low.
Patient is taking metformin 500mg twice a day.
Patient's blood pressure is 120/70.
Patient has wheezing and rhonchi present on the right side of the lung.
Patient has diminished breath sounds in the right side of the lung.
Patient has a two/over/success-o-lic ejection murmur.
Patient's abdomen is soft.
Patient has one plus nonpitting edema on both lower extremities.
Patient has shortness of breath.
Patient is coughing up blood.
Patient has elevated blood sugar.
Patient has low blood pressure.
Patient may have a possible lung infection.
Patient will have a Chest X-ray.
Patient will be referred to the Emergency Department for further bloodwork, chest x-ray, and CT scan.
Patient will have a Hemoglobin A1c repeat.
Patient's metformin dose will be increased to 750mg twice daily.
Norvasc will be held until patient's blood pressure is stabilized.
Patient will have a follow-up visit in 3 weeks for diabetes management.
